summary: I lived in a refugee camp in Thailand for two years before coming to America. It was a difficult time for my family, but it was our temporary home after leaving Burma. Thailand was where I stayed with my father and sister before we moved to the United States. Although life there was challenging, it was a crucial part of my journey to a better future.

I came to the United States of America with my father and sister after living in Thailand. Since arriving, I have been living in St. Paul, Minnesota for about ten months. America excites me because of its wonderful buildings, bridges, and the chance to see snow for the first time. I know that here I have opportunities to go to school, find a job, and access healthcare, which I couldn't have in Burma due to the civil war.

I have been living in St. Paul, Minnesota for ten months since coming to America. It was my first time seeing snow, which was exciting but also made me change my lifestyle to wearing thick coats. Sometimes I feel bored in the winter and don't like going outside, but I appreciate the opportunities here, like going to school and finding a job. St. Paul has become my new home where I am trying to improve my life.

I come from Burma, where life was very difficult because of the civil war that often disrupted events in my village. I couldn't go to school or have a permanent job there, which made it hard to improve my living standards. That’s why my family left Burma and eventually ended up in a refugee camp in Thailand before coming to America. Burma is where my story began, but it was not a place where I could build a better future.
